packs.name: engineering
packs.description: Coding standards, security, backend architecture, web app implementation, Firebase services
packs.base: Universal coding standards, security, testing, naming, error handling, architectural principles

subSkills.name: backend-principles
subSkills.description: Backend/API/serverless architecture, production readiness, rate limiting

subSkills.name: web-app-frontend
subSkills.description: Web apps with React, Vue, GSAP, ShadCN, Tailwind, TypeScript: state, animation, security

subSkills.name: firebase-cloud-functions
subSkills.description: Cloud Functions for Firebase (2nd gen), idempotency, scaling, secrets

subSkills.name: firebase-firestore
subSkills.description: Firestore provisioning, security rules, queries, indexing

packs.name: design
packs.description: Design system fundamentals, frontend principles, web/mobile design, onboarding UX
packs.base: Universal design system: spacing (4px grid), typography, colors (HSL/OKLCH), visual hierarchy, layout, accessibility, emotional design

subSkills.name: frontend-principles
subSkills.description: Universal frontend rules: zero hardcoded values, token architecture, translations, components, a11y

subSkills.name: design-web
subSkills.description: Web design: responsive layouts, landing pages, animation, conversion optimization

subSkills.name: design-mobile
subSkills.description: Mobile design: haptics, native transitions, widgets, App Store, gamification

subSkills.name: onboarding-design
subSkills.description: Onboarding flows, paywalls, signup funnels, first-run UX, rating prompts

packs.name: growth
packs.description: Load when working on user acquisition, retention strategy, push notifications, TikTok/social distribution, content farming, paywall optimization, LTV, monetization, or organic viral loops
packs.base: App growth strategy: retention engineering, distribution, content-led growth, VSC framework, monetization, viral loops

subSkills.name: performance-marketing
subSkills.description: Paid ads, Meta/Facebook Ads, ROAS optimization, audience funnels, budget sizing

subSkills.name: lean-analytics-experiments
subSkills.description: Experiment design, MVPs, A/B testing, hypothesis validation, statistical methods

subSkills.name: lean-analytics-metrics
subSkills.description: Product analytics, KPIs, event schemas, Mixpanel, cohort/funnel analysis

packs.name: brand-voice
packs.description: Load when writing emails, proposals, presentations, LinkedIn posts, sales content, pitch decks, or any brand-aligned content. Also triggers on brand discovery, guideline generation, brand content audits, and voice enforcement.
packs.base: Brand voice enforcement: load guidelines from _dream_context/core/, apply voice constants and tone flexes, validate content, explain brand choices

subSkills.name: discover-brand
subSkills.description: Orchestrate brand material discovery across enterprise platforms (Notion, Confluence, Google Drive, Box, SharePoint, Figma, Gong, Granola, Slack)

subSkills.name: guideline-generation
subSkills.description: Generate brand voice guidelines from documents, transcripts, discovery reports, or direct input. Saves as core file.

packs.name: council
packs.description: Load when the user wants to debate a non-trivial decision, asks for a second opinion, says 'help me decide', 'run this by council', or invokes /council. Orchestrates 3–10 persona sub-agents across multiple rounds, then a synthesizer writes a final decision report.
packs.base: Structured multi-agent debate: orchestrator dispatches persona sub-agents in parallel rounds, reads only exec summaries, then dispatches a synthesizer for the final report. File-based IPC keeps the main agent's context light.

subSkills.name: debate-protocol
subSkills.description: Detailed CLI protocol reference: 14 sub-commands, required report subsections, failure modes, file layout, context-budget discipline.

packs.name: multi-review
packs.description: Load when the user invokes /multi-review, says 'multi-reviewer', 'review with a team', 'review this PR with specialists', 'team review', 'thorough review', or otherwise asks for a multi-aspect code review (security + cloud-functions + frontend + edge-cases) of a diff or branch. Routes the diff to relevant specialist sub-agents in parallel, then the main agent dedupes findings into one greptile-style report.
packs.base: Orchestrated specialist code review: router classifies the diff by tier (Trivial/Lite/Full) + domain tags, dispatches niche specialists in parallel, main agent dedupes findings and emits one greptile-style report with READY_TO_MERGE / NEEDS_ATTENTION / NEEDS_WORK verdict. Each specialist auto-loads declared dreamcontext skills via YAML frontmatter.

subSkills.name: reviewer-shared
subSkills.description: Shared rubric every specialist obeys: severity ladder (Critical/Major/Minor/Nit), output format, what-NOT-to-flag rules, verdict mapping, skill-loading and context discipline.

crossPackDeps: design (frontend specialist loads design tokens + a11y)

standalone.name: business-idea-discovery
standalone.description: Load when discovering business ideas, validating market demand, mining pain points from Reddit/forums, researching competitors on Flippa/Acquire/Sensor Tower, checking Google Trends, evaluating marketability, or scoping an MVP. Full pre-build discovery pipeline.

standalone.name: business-idea-validation
standalone.description: Sequential stage-gate pipeline for deep business idea validation. 9 stages, 6 specialist sub-agents, kill gates between every stage. Takes an idea through problem discovery, customer analysis, market sizing, competitive intelligence, customer simulation, synthesis, GTM strategy, financial viability, to a scored Go/No-Go decision.

subSkills.name: stage-definitions
subSkills.description: All 9 stages: inputs, outputs, success criteria, kill signals, and confidence scoring rubric

crossPackDeps: business-idea-discovery (find ideas fast, then validate deeply)

standalone.name: meta-marketing
standalone.description: Load when planning, launching, or optimizing Meta (Facebook/Instagram) ad campaigns. Covers campaign structure, audience, creative strategy, post-launch optimization, and common failure modes.
standalone.corpus_status: 9 videos · 4 speakers · draft · agent roster: 2 active (strategy, monitor) + 1 stubbed (creative)

standalone.name: system-prompts
standalone.description: Load when writing/reviewing system prompts, configuring AI agents, designing instruction hierarchies, optimizing for Claude/GPT/Gemini/DeepSeek, prompt injection defense, multi-stage agent flows (ReAct, AlphaCodium, LATS), or context engineering

agents.name: reviewer
agents.description: Critical code reviewer and final quality gate. Only flags Critical and Major issues (security, data loss, memory leaks, breaking changes). Returns PASS or FAIL.

agents.name: discover-brand
agents.description: Autonomous 4-phase brand material search across enterprise MCP platforms

agents.name: council-persona
agents.description: A persona sub-agent in a council debate. Loads persona + peer cross-context via CLI, thinks from the assigned perspective, writes a structured round report. Dispatched in parallel, one per persona per round.

agents.name: council-synthesizer
agents.description: Reads every report + persona + research in a council debate and writes the final decision report with traced Whys, position-shift timeline, and minority views.

agents.name: marketing-strategy
agents.description: Strategy Optimizer for Meta ads — plans cohort hypotheses (shape-validated), drafts campaign briefs grounded in the meta-marketing skill-pack corpus, always asks for budget, never launches.

agents.name: marketing-monitor
agents.description: Performance Monitor for live Meta campaigns — reads insights, applies post-launch rules from account-ops.md §4, writes hypothesis-ledger entries to per-day learnings, recommends moves but never auto-mutates.

agents.name: marketing-creative
agents.description: Creative Director for Meta ads — STUBBED in v0. Refuses all requests until marketing.creative_director.enabled = true. v1 will merge brainstorming + copy + asset generation.

agents.name: review-router
agents.description: Classifies a code diff for the multi-reviewer system. Categorizes by size tier (Trivial/Lite/Full), tags affected domains, outputs JSON dispatch plan telling the main agent which specialists to invoke and which files to scope to each. Always runs first in the multi-review flow.

agents.name: review-security
agents.description: Security specialist in the multi-review team. Reviews ONLY for security defects — exploitable vulnerabilities, secret leakage, auth/authz gaps, injection, SSRF/CSRF/XSS, env-var exposure, weak crypto, insecure deserialization. Outputs a bounded greptile-style report.

agents.name: review-cloud-functions
agents.description: Cloud Functions specialist in the multi-review team. Reviews ONLY Firebase Cloud Functions and serverless function changes — idempotency, infinite-loop risk, scaling/concurrency, cold-start cost, retry semantics, secrets handling, unbounded fan-out, billing footguns, trigger-loop hazards.

agents.name: review-frontend
agents.description: Frontend specialist in the multi-review team. Reviews ONLY frontend changes (React/Vue/Svelte/Next/web components/CSS) — file-size scalability, hook correctness, accessibility, bundle bloat, render perf, state management, design-token discipline, i18n, and frontend-specific security (XSS sinks, token storage).

agents.name: review-edge-cases
agents.description: Edge-case and production-risk specialist in the multi-review team. The paranoid one. Enumerates failure modes the other specialists don't own — empty/null/undefined inputs, off-by-one, concurrency, partial successes, retries, network failures, clock skew, timezone bugs, locale assumptions, state machine gaps, error swallowing. Always included for tier >= Lite.

agents.name: biv-researcher
agents.description: Research specialist for BIV pipeline. Executes Stage 1 (Problem Discovery — Reddit mining, forum analysis, Google Trends) and Stage 4 (Competitive Intelligence — competitor dossiers, gap analysis, 2x2 matrix). Heavy web research.

agents.name: biv-customer-analyst
agents.description: Customer analysis specialist for BIV pipeline. Executes Stage 2 (Customer Analysis — segmentation, personas, beachhead selection) and Stage 5 (AI-simulated Customer Validation from forum data).

agents.name: biv-market-analyst
agents.description: Market sizing specialist for BIV pipeline. Executes Stage 3 (Market Sizing — TAM/SAM/SOM via top-down and bottom-up analysis, ambition-level gating).

agents.name: biv-strategist
agents.description: Strategy specialist for BIV pipeline. Executes Stage 6 (Synthesis & Hypothesis — opportunity snapshot, riskiest assumptions, testable hypothesis, RICE features), Stage 7 (GTM Strategy — channels, messaging, first-100-users plan), and optional Lean Canvas capstone.

agents.name: biv-financial-analyst
agents.description: Financial viability specialist for BIV pipeline. Executes Stage 8 (Year 1 P&L, unit economics — CAC/LTV/LTV:CAC, sensitivity analysis, Year 2 outlook).

agents.name: biv-decision-gate
agents.description: Final decision specialist for BIV pipeline. Executes Stage 9 (Go/No-Go — scores 8 dimensions across Desirability/Feasibility/Viability, applies risk penalties, produces verdict: GO/PIVOT/NO-GO with specific next steps).
